main_useful_patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved
uncertainties: Do not construct success_like preference pairs because no high-confidence success_like trajectory exists.
Do not treat partial_progress as success_like; only weak preference pairs are allowed.

forbidden_changes: do not only scale all coefficients
do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not treat noisy partial_progress as success_like
do not use local_repair when training is declining or lessons repeat
required_changes: explicitly avoid known failures: early_failure, low_progress_survival

preserve useful patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved; TRAINING TREND WARNING: mean returns declining: [-4.629808684701914, -3.5808512721411407, -0.31125703256694004, 166.0559504379101, 20.93371329551888, -1.9433229388047928, 3.5274976761155123, 0.5124704769900237]; structural mutation is required, do NOT use local_repair

gate or remove components: crash_penalty, gated_timeout_penalty, low_progress_survival_gate, stability_penalty, terminal_failure_penalty, timeout_penalty, ungated_contact_bonus, ungated_survival_bonus
preserve or strengthen components: approach_progress, far_approach_progress, leg_bonus, leg_contact_bonus, near_stage_stability, progress_delta, safe_landing_bonus, terminal_failure_penalty
structural refactoring required: training evidence shows local_repair is insufficient

rollback_decision.reason: Hard rollback is handled by RollbackManager, not Analyzer.

self_evaluation_lesson: Trajectory memory supports preference-guided mutation, but partial progress must remain distinct from success.

previous_selection_used.allowed_decision: Use weak preference pairs to select a candidate for long training with low-confidence warning.

previous_selection_used.reason: Selected valid candidate using weak preference evidence.

observed_outcome.main_success_signal: selected candidate produces only partial_progress trajectories, eliminating early failures and low_progress_survival in its own evaluation

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: no success_like trajectories; safe_landing_bonus never fires due to overly strict condition
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: restructured safe_landing_bonus to provide partial credit for sub-conditions and added intermediate rewards for leg contact and stability near pad
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: candidate generates 50 partial_progress trajectories but still no success_like achieved; safe_landing_bonus remains insufficient to trigger final terminal condition

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Local repair that provides intermediate rewards for progress toward terminal condition can eliminate early failures and produce consistent partial progress, but bridging the gap to success_like requires aligning reward with the exact terminal state rather than intermediate approximations; pure partial-progress dominance does not imply success readiness.
future_guidance: preserve approach_progress component as it drives partial_progress
avoid treating partial_progress as success_like in preference construction
strengthen terminal condition reward with progressive relaxation or shaping to encourage the final landing step
consider using curriculum learning or gradual tightening of safe_landing condition to guide policy from partial_progress to success_like

archive_update.reason: Candidate selected for long training and added to elite archive; no rollback needed as conditions not met.

uncertainties: No success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed
Selection based on weak pairwise evidence, conclusions may not generalize to success behavior
True success may require a fundamentally different reward structure not yet explored

observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: no_success_like_trajectories; partial_progress count dropped from 50 to 17 in long training
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: memory coverage still shows 124 partial_progress trajectories, but candidate training regressed

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: parent reward consistently generates partial_progress (124 in memory, 50 in previous training) but no success_like trajectories; plateau in partial_progress conversion
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: local_repair mutation to strengthen terminal condition rewards (safe_landing_bonus) and reduce ineffective components (stability_penalty, crash_penalty, etc.) while preserving approach_progress and near_stage_stability
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: long training produced only 17 partial_progress, 20 ambiguous, and 9 early_failure; training return dropped from 166 to 20.9; no success_like trajectories emerged; the repair disrupted partial_progress generation without achieving progress toward success

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Local repair that simultaneously removes multiple components and modifies terminal conditions can destabilize the existing partial_progress behavior; preserving the ability to generate partial_progress is a prerequisite before attempting to bridge to success; incremental, single-component changes with validation of partial_progress retention are safer.
future_guidance: preserve approach_progress component as it is the primary driver of partial_progress
avoid simultaneous removal or gating of multiple components; test each change individually
before introducing new success mechanisms, ensure the reward function maintains at least the same number of partial_progress trajectories as the parent
investigate ambiguous trajectories: they may indicate reward misalignment and should be minimized
do not treat ambiguous trajectories as progress in preference construction
consider using a conservative mutation that adds a mild success bonus without altering existing components
re-evaluate the necessity of removing components that were previously helpful (e.g., crash_penalty, stability_penalty) as their removal may degrade partial_progress

archive_update.reason: Candidate was selected, trained, and added to elite archive despite regression; next round should consider reverting to a parent that preserved partial_progress (e.g., round2 or round3 elite) and apply more conservative mutations.

uncertainties: The regression in partial_progress may be due to stochasticity in training or the mutation itself; the candidate's TAGE score on memory was positive (0.63), so the memory evaluation did not predict the training outcome
No success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed
The high number of ambiguous trajectories (20) indicates the reward function may be poorly calibrated for the policy's actual behavior
Decision level was weak_pairwise_selection, so all conclusions are tentative and may not generalize

observed_outcome.main_success_signal: partial_progress count increased to 141 in memory and 31 in training, early_failure eliminated

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: partial_progress regression (from 50 to 17) and emergence of ambiguous trajectories due to aggressive local_repair in round4
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: progress_conditioned mutation preserving approach_progress and near_stage_stability while gating crash_penalty, stability_penalty, terminal_failure_penalty, and timeout_penalty
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: partial_progress count recovered to 31, low_progress_survival increased to 19, no early_failure; still no success_like trajectories

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Progress-conditioned mutation that preserves core components like approach_progress can restore partial_progress after disruption, but converting partial_progress into success_like behavior requires explicit terminal condition rewards beyond intermediate progress signals.
future_guidance: preserve approach_progress component as primary driver of partial_progress
avoid simultaneous removal or gating of multiple components; make incremental changes
strengthen or add terminal success condition rewards with progressive relaxation or shaping
investigate and improve consistency of mid_approach_progress and mid_stability components
do not treat partial_progress as success_like in preference construction
consider curriculum learning to gradually tighten success criteria

uncertainties: No success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed

observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: No success_like trajectories; new components have low pairwise alignment with memory preferences
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: Partial_progress trajectories remain dominant (172 out of 280), training return improved from -1.94 to 3.53

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: Parent reward generated consistent partial_progress but never success_like due to overly strict safe_landing_bonus and penalties (timeout_penalty, terminal_failure_penalty) that penalized partial_progress more than failures.
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: Relaxed safe_landing_bonus to intermediate thresholds (intermediate_safe_bonus), gated timeout_penalty and terminal_failure_penalty to apply only when no progress, preserved approach_progress, near_stage_stability, leg_contact_bonus.
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: Partial_progress maintained; training return improved but no success_like trajectories. New components (intermediate_safe_bonus, gated_timeout_penalty, gated_failure_penalty) show low or mixed pairwise alignment with memory preferences, indicating the repair may not effectively steer toward success-like behavior.

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Incremental terminal reward relaxation can maintain partial_progress but may introduce components that misalign with desired behavior; further refinement of gating thresholds and bonus structure is needed.
future_guidance: Preserve approach_progress component
Re-evaluate intermediate_safe_bonus conditions to improve pairwise alignment
Ensure gated_timeout_penalty and gated_failure_penalty do not over-reward low-quality trajectories
Consider progressive shaping of terminal conditions rather than discrete thresholds
Avoid adding new components without testing their alignment on memory preferences

archive_update.reason: Selected candidate has high TAGE score (0.822) and was promoted to elite archive; continue with this reward for further refinement.

uncertainties: No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; weak pairwise evidence may not generalize to success behavior

Training return improvement may be partially due to stochasticity

observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: no_success_like_generated, partial_progress generation dropped from 50 to 14 in training trajectories, gated_timeout_penalty still penalizes some partial_progress
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: failure_avoidance remains high (mean reward on failures ~2.2), approach_progress and near_stage_stability still strongly discriminate partial_progress from failures

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: parent reward generated 50 partial_progress trajectories but no success_like; terminal bonuses too strict, gated_timeout_penalty penalized partial_progress
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: local_repair mutation: adjusted thresholds of gated_timeout_penalty and possibly other components, but did not restructure safe_landing_bonus or intermediate_safe_bonus to progressive shaping as required
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: partial_progress generation dropped to 14 out of 50 training trajectories; still no success_like; gated_timeout_penalty consistency improved from 0.225 to 0.312 but still penalizes some partial_progress; failure avoidance maintained

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Local threshold tuning of gated penalties can reduce partial_progress generation if not accompanied by structural changes to terminal bonuses; progressive shaping of safe landing bonuses is necessary to bridge to success_like without sacrificing partial_progress.
future_guidance: restructure safe_landing_bonus and intermediate_safe_bonus using progressive thresholds that become more stringent over time or state
ensure gated_timeout_penalty only applies to trajectories with negligible progress (delta_dist < 0 and curr_dist > 0.5) to avoid penalizing partial_progress
preserve approach_progress and near_stage_stability components as-is
avoid removing or over-aggressively tuning components that drive partial_progress generation
consider adding a continuous terminal bonus that activates when lander is close and stable
